allies.rationale_text: Systems Thinking helps expected value identify the full variable structure that must be included in the payoff calculation.
allies.source_quote: Systems thinking provides the comprehensive structure ... necessary to ensure all variables contributing to the EV are correctly identified.

allies.rationale_text: Bayesian updating strengthens expected value by adjusting probabilities as new evidence arrives.
allies.source_quote: By using Bayesian techniques, EV calculations become adaptive, integrating new evidence to update likelihoods.

allies.rationale_text: Game-theory-payoffs uses expected-value logic to account for adversary reactions in strategic choice.
allies.source_quote: Game Theory models the payoffs (which are essentially Expected Values) of strategic moves, anticipating how an adversary will react.

allies.rationale_text: First principles thinking keeps the expected-value calculation anchored to core facts instead of inherited assumptions.
allies.source_quote: Applying EV requires isolating the fundamental facts and desired outcomes, stripping away conventional assumptions.

antagonists.rationale_text: Hindsight bias corrupts expected-value calibration by making past probability estimates feel less uncertain than they were — outcomes that are now known appear to have always been more probable, distorting the feedback signal for improving future EV assessments.
antagonists.source_quote: Affect heuristic and hindsight bias remain source-real antagonists.

antagonists.rationale_text: Cognitive biases work against expected value by replacing slow payoff analysis with intuitive shortcuts and substitutions.
antagonists.source_quote: EV demands slow, analytical System 2 thought. People often use immediate mental models ... rather than engaging in the arduous EV calculation.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Expected-value conflicts with representativeness-heuristic when vivid outcomes override probability-weighted evaluation.
structured_tensions.source_quote: expected-value conflicts with representativeness-heuristic (representativeness heuristic) when vivid outcomes override probability-weighted evaluation.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Expected-value conflicts with sunk-cost-fallacy when prior spend distorts forward-looking payoff comparisons.
structured_tensions.source_quote: expected-value conflicts with sunk-cost-fallacy (sunk cost fallacy) when prior spend distorts forward-looking payoff comparisons.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Expected-value conflicts with false-precision-avoidance when numerical rigor is applied to conjectural inputs that have not earned it.
structured_tensions.source_quote: expected-value conflicts with false-precision-avoidance (false precision avoidance) when precise probability estimates are assigned to conjectural data, creating a veneer of mathematical rigor over speculative inputs.

curation_notes.summary: Expected Value scales cleanly in this layer because the source already presents a strong ally lattice and explicit structured tensions.
donor_drops: Did not import donor relation overlays or higher-order topology ideas.
open_questions: Hindsight-bias wired in Task 3.3 sweep via new canonical model.
Affect heuristic remains deferred — no canonical model id yet.

antagonists: The raw source explicitly names affect heuristic and hindsight bias, but those were omitted here because the current canonical target-id surface is not yet clean enough.
